content: GitHub Actions coverage reporting for Node.js: generate lcov.info via c8 or node --experimental-test-coverage --test-reporter=lcov, then upload with codecov/codecov-action@v4 or coverallsapp/github-action@v2. Codecov requires CODECOV_TOKEN secret for private repos (public repos may work without). Coveralls requires COVERALLS_REPO_TOKEN. Both actions accept lcov.info. For open-source projects without a coverage service, lcov-reporter-action can post coverage as a PR comment. Summary-only reporting (no external service) is possible with node --experimental-test-coverage alone — it prints line/branch/function percentages to stdout, which appears in CI logs. This is the lowest-friction approach: zero secrets, zero external services, visible in Actions log.
